look.description:
Study.
This is the old study used by the gravedigger who once owned this cottage,
where he read up his craft. It is decorated in sombre colours, and the
windows are small and dirty. On the south wall is a large bookcase reaching
up to the ceiling, made of an enchanted oak.
The bookcase has been shifted aside to allow passage behind it.
*

IN.description:
Hall.
You are standing in an oddly shaped hall. To the south is a doorway, the east
is an archway, and some dark forbidding stairs lead upwards to the southeast.
Immediately to the west is a fitted wardrobe, and some eerie, granite steps
to the southwest lead downwards to the cellar.
The kitchen door is locked shut.
*

open wardrobe.description:
It's all double dutch to me mate!
*

e.description:
Study.
This is the old study used by the gravedigger who once owned this cottage,
where he read up his craft. It is decorated in sombre colours, and the
windows are small and dirty. On the south wall is a large bookcase reaching
up to the ceiling, made of an enchanted oak.
The bookcase stands flush against the wall.
*

s.description:
The room is too dark to see anything.
*

open door.description:
The door is locked shut, and needs keys to open it.
*

val libram.description:
The base value of the Libram is 10, and the current value is 17.
*

sw.description:
In front of hut.
You are standing in front of a ramshackle hut. Behind you is a field of gorse.
Further on to the south you can see a fast flowing river. To the east you can
see a range of mountains wreathed in mist and to the west is a small cottage.
*

in.description:
Hall.
You are standing in an oddly shaped hall. To the south is a doorway, the east
is an archway, and some dark forbidding stairs lead upwards to the southeast.
Immediately to the west is a fitted wardrobe, and some eerie, granite steps
to the southwest lead downwards to the cellar.
The kitchen door is locked shut.
*

out.description:
In front of hut.
It is raining.
*

n.description:
Study.
The bookcase has been shifted aside to allow passage behind it.
*

look bookcase.description:
Study.
This is the old study used by the gravedigger who once owned this cottage,
where he read up his craft. It is decorated in sombre colours, and the
windows are small and dirty. On the south wall is a large bookcase reaching
up to the ceiling, made of an enchanted oak.
The bookcase stands flush against the wall.
*

exits.description:
swamp		Lounge.
out		Lounge.
west		Lounge.
*

val fur.description:
The base value of the Fur is 7, and the current value is 12.
*

exits.swamp: A deaf, dumb, blind, lame beggar sits despondently nearby

A deaf, dumb, blind, lame beggar sits de.description:
You are attacked by the beggar!
*The violence of a cross from the beggar sends you sideways.
Yet you compose, and stagger forward into the melee.
*swamp
You can't leave in the middle of a fight unless you flee in some direction!
*Your feeble retaliatory swing at the beggar is easily dodged.
*You narrowly shrug off a pathetic cross from the beggar.
*Your weak swing is parried effortlessly by the beggar.
*You simply side-step a limp forehand by the beggar.
*You hit out at the beggar with a powerful clout!
*You effortlessly evade a tame slash by the beggar.
*You take aim at the beggar with a fierce whack!
*You simply parry a dreadful lunge by the beggar.
*

flee w.description:
Gorse.
You are in a tangled mass of prickly gorse.
A mighty woodsman's axe lies on the ground here.
*

move bookcase.description:
The bookcase slides aside easily revealing an opening.
*

Elizabethan tearoom.description:
This cosy, Tudor room is where all British Legends adventures start. Its
exposed oak beams and soft, velvet-covered furnishings provide it with the
ideal atmosphere in which to relax before venturing out into that strange,
timeless realm. A sense of decency and decorum prevails, and a feeling of
kinship with those who, like you, seek their destiny in The Land. There are
exits in all directions, each of which leads into a wisping, magical mist of
obvious teleportative properties...
*

Fitted wardrobe.description:
You are inside the fitted wardrobe. The only apparent exit is to the east,
into a hallway.
*

Lounge.description:
There is a fire burning merrily in the grate, its cheerful glare spreads a
delightful air of homeliness.
*

Study.description:
On the bookcase lies a single tome: "Articles of magic".
The bookcase stands flush against the wall.
*

South pasture.description:
All about you is a pleasant pasture. To the north is a fast-flowing stream,
and to the south is forest. Westwards is a thundering waterfall.
A deaf, dumb, blind, lame beggar sits despondently nearby.
*

Dense forest.description:
In amongst the trees is an evil-looking dryad, scowling menacingly.
*

Pine forest.description:
A white, winged stallion stands majestically before you.
*

Bandstand.description:
You are standing in a bandstand in the midst of the pine forest. All around
you are trees, denser in the easterly directions than in the westerly ones.
The bandstand hasn't been used for some time, obviously with it being in the
middle of a forest, and in places its paint is peeling, but it is
nevertheless a surprisingly fine example.
*

Flower garden.description:
A gorgeous hyacinth has been picked and left here.
A curious herb gives off a sweet odour nearby.
*

Pond in swamp.description:
A bloated toad croaks incessantly by the side of the water.
*

Sundial in pine forest.description:
This is part of a large pine forest. To the northeast, the forest opens up
onto a magical glade, but in other directions is more forest, some of it too
dense to allow passage. Before you stands an old, stone sundial, overgrown
with ivy.
The sundial has no gnomon, so cannot tell the time.
*

Rocky Beach.description:
A seagull flutters about you, aimlessly.
*

Foothills.description:
There is a small hole in the ground, about 5 inches across. It bends too
sharply for you to see anything down it.
*

Woodsman's hut.description:
An empty space on the wall is marked, in an unsteady but loving hand, by the
word "MOOSE".
*

You're already keeping key.description:
*
A seagull flutters about you, aimlessly.
*
The Gull has just left.
*

Bottom of waterfall.description:
A seagull flutters about you, aimlessly.
*

Forbidding tunnel.description:
The iron grate is slid open.
*

Jetty.description:
There is a lobster pot here.
The pot contains nothing
*

Deck.description:
A sailable coracle large enough for one has been beached (?) here.
*

Crow's nest.description:
The golden earrings of some forgotten pirate lie here.
*

Hold.description:
A raft, large enough to carry one person, has been left here.
Lying on the floor is some fine silk.
*

Room HOME.description:
(Hide)
Wizards' room.
Morpheus's almanac is here, containing the following advice:
"ERASE is now fixed. Stopped working in 2016... <Viktor>"
"For anyone interested, I saved a backup of the almanac here: https://tinyurl.com/mud1-wiz-almanac"
"I agree it was historical. Viktor, any chance it can be restored? <Bluelight>"
"I noticed that my listed email in the forum Wizard list was an old abandoned one.  Is there any way to update that to Axiomelf@gmail.com  <Aegtwaet> (1/24)"
"Hope you're all well, saying hi after 13 years! Is it possible to list my email as espressoneil@gmail.com in the wizard forum so anyone can contact me if they like? Extremely belated"
"feel free to email me loucypher1403609@gmail.com <Morpheus>"
"new player BOB[103304,2144] "
"Just watched Maeglin fry the want attempt to zap the pool. Pretty indicative of where his development is at."
"Maeglin [102411,7112] has alts on at the same time dellusion [70534,7112] also  jorbert [103655,7112] and Alios [70222,7112] <Morpheus>"
"Barnaby [72043,7117] online with alts. He has 1 alt per last letter RKJ[a-z] [xxxxxx,7117] Rkjz [74427,7117] Suggestion LIMBO??? <Morpheus>"
"I have sent Barnaby [72043,7117] and RetSech[72043,7117] to Limbo <Morph>"
"I have agreed to a consession. The portcullis and Shrine need multi's online to solve, So for these puzzles only may there be Multi's If there are no Other players<Morph>"
"I disagree. Players should rather find others to joi"
"--n them online."
"^who? and when you are online, tell them that. There a few players and only 2 people play, this leaves little chance for them to experiment. Hence my comment,"
"Having given Barnaby a little leeway, to learn the Shrine, he has decided to continue to abuse it by Using his alts as a login watchers sitting in Start, the Elizabeth Tea room. this is something I have given a Warning about.<Morph."
"we should put each of his ALTS in Limbo. Maeglin has started playing more, So they can work together. Trust broken and in light of this, <morph>"
"Maeglin, Maeglen  [102411,7112]  etal also Start watching<morph>"
(----*)
